# On the True Shape of Our World: A Mariner's Testimony

*Being the account and argument of Captain Elias Thorne, formerly of the merchant vessel* Meridian's Hope, *set down in the year of our Lord 1874*

---

I have stood upon the decks of ships in every ocean this world possesses. I have watched the sun rise over the Indian waters and set behind the peaks of Patagonia. I have felt the sting of Arctic wind and the oppressive stillness of the equatorial doldrums. And I tell you now, with all the conviction a man's soul can muster: the Earth upon which we live and sail is a flat plane, and the globe to which our learned gentlemen cling is the grandest deception ever perpetrated upon the minds of honest men.

Hear me out, reader, before you cast this pamphlet into the fire.

I was raised, as you were, to believe in the spinning ball. My schoolmasters drew it upon their slates; my first captain swore by it. For thirty years at sea I accepted it as a child accepts the existence of angels — because my betters had told me so. But the sea, I have come to learn, is a harsher and more honest schoolmaster than any man in a cloister. She answers only to what *is*, not to what learned societies in London and Paris have decreed *ought to be*.

Consider, first, the plainest fact any man with eyes in his head may verify: **water lies flat.** Pour it into a basin, and it settles level. Pour it into a lake, and it stretches level from shore to shore. Pour it across a continent in the form of a great river or inland sea, and — I submit — it remains level still. For what force in nature has ever been observed to bend a body of still water into a convex hill? None. Not one. The notion is contrary to every experience of every farmer, every miller, every canal-builder who has ever lived.

And here I come to the evidence that ought, in any rational age, to have ended this controversy forever: the experiments conducted upon the **Old Bedford Level** in the fens of Cambridgeshire. That placid canal runs in a perfectly straight line for some six miles between Welney and Welches Dam — an uninterrupted ribbon of still water, unbroken by lock or bend. Mr. Samuel Rowbotham, a man of patient and meticulous temper, placed himself at one end with a good telescope held but eight inches above the surface. At the far end, six miles distant, a boat was rowed bearing a flag five feet high.

By the spherical doctrine, that flag ought to have been hidden beneath some sixteen feet of watery curvature — sunk utterly from view, as if drowned. And yet, reader, the flag stood plainly visible through the glass, from bottom to top, along the whole length of the canal. The water had not curved. It had not bent. It lay flat, as water does, as water must, as water always has.

I have repeated observations of like kind myself, across the Bay of Bengal and along the coasts of the Baltic, sighting distant lighthouses which the globe-theory insists should lie far below the horizon, yet which stand plainly visible to any mariner with a stout telescope. The seamen of my acquaintance know this. We speak of it quietly in dockside taverns, though few will set their names to it in print for fear of ridicule from the academies.

And why such fear? Ask yourself: **who profits from the globe?** The Royal Society, drawing its pensions from the Crown. The astronomers, whose livelihoods depend upon a cosmos of spinning orbs. The admiralties and their navigational monopolies. The governments, which find it convenient that common men should imagine themselves clinging precariously to a hurtling ball, dizzy and powerless, rather than standing firm upon a stable plain that is plainly their inheritance. A frightened population is a governable one. A population that trusts its own senses is not so easily led.

They will object that ships disappear hull-first over the horizon. I have seen this illusion myself, and I have also seen it reversed with a good telescope, the vanished hull restored in an instant to plain sight — which no true curvature could permit. The phenomenon is an effect of perspective and the thickening of air at great distance, nothing more, and any surveyor honest in his trade will admit as much in private company.

They will object that men have circumnavigated the globe. I answer: a compass held over a flat disc, turning always eastward, shall in time return its bearer to his starting point just as surely as one held over a ball. Circumnavigation proves only that the Earth is *finite in the east-west sense*, not that it is spherical.

They will object that the stars wheel differently in southern latitudes. I answer: so they must, when viewed from different positions beneath a vast and moving celestial canopy. The appearances are consistent with many geometries; the globe is but one, and not the most natural.

They will invoke gravitation — that mysterious and invisible glue by which Sir Isaac supposedly holds oceans to the underside of a spinning ball without spilling a drop. I say: show me the force. Show me the experiment, free of presupposition, that demonstrates a ball-Earth rather than assuming it. They cannot. They begin with the conclusion and adorn it afterward with mathematics so ornate that the plain man, cowed by symbols he cannot read, surrenders his own good sense.

I do not ask you to take my word. I ask you to do what I did. Go to a long canal, or a frozen lake, or any great stretch of still water. Bring a good glass. Bring a friend with a flag. Measure for yourself. The Earth will answer you, as she answered me, in the language she has always spoken to those willing to listen: **she is flat, and she is still, and she is ours.**

The globe is a painted curtain. Pull it aside.

I remain, your faithful servant and a sincere friend to truth,

**Captain Elias Thorne**
*Written at Portsmouth, this twelfth day of October*

# The Plane Truth: A Manifesto

### by Captain Elias Thorne

They told me, as they told you, that we cling like insects to the side of a spinning ball hurtling through an endless vacuum at speeds the mind cannot fathom. They told me this before I could read, before I could question, before I had ever laid my own eyes upon the horizon and asked it to tell me the truth. I believed them, once. I no longer do. My name is Elias Thorne, and I have spent the better part of thirty years — on ships, in cockpits, on the ice of the southern rim — learning to see again. What follows is not speculation. It is testimony.

**Look at the horizon. Truly look.**

I have flown at thirty-five thousand feet in the cockpits of 747s from Singapore to San Francisco, from Johannesburg to London. I have pressed my face to the glass at the forward windows, and I have watched the horizon for hour after hour. And do you know what I have seen? A line. A perfectly straight, flat, level line, stretching from one wingtip to the other, rising to meet the aircraft as it climbs. If I were truly perched seven miles above a ball eight thousand miles across, that horizon would curve before me like the rim of a bowl. It does not. It never has. No pilot I have ever flown with — and I have flown with many — has seen it curve. The windows of commercial aircraft are not shaped to deceive us. The air is not bending the light. The horizon is flat because the Earth beneath it is flat.

**Consider the rivers.**

The Nile flows northward for over four thousand miles, from the highlands of East Africa to the Mediterranean Sea. Across that vast distance, it descends, in total, perhaps five hundred feet. Five hundred feet across four thousand miles. I ask you — I beg you — to do the arithmetic that the schoolteachers dare not. On a sphere of the dimensions we are told, the curvature across four thousand miles would plunge downward by hundreds of miles. A river cannot flow up and over the bulge of a ball and then back down again; water does not climb hills unbidden. And yet the Nile flows, serene and level, as it has since the Pharaohs. The Mississippi flows. The Amazon flows. The Volga flows. Every great river on this plane is a spirit level laid across the face of the world, and every one of them reads true.

**Consider the canals and the railways.**

The engineers who cut the Suez, who dug the old Bedford Level, who laid the transcontinental rails across the American West — not one of them calculated for curvature. Not one. The surveyor's transit is set level and sighted straight, and the earth is found to meet it straight. If we truly lived upon a globe, every bridge would be misaligned, every long canal would require a downward tilt at its midpoint, and every rail line of a hundred miles would drop thousands of feet below the level of its origin. None of this is done. None of it has ever been done. The men who build the world treat it as flat because it is flat.

**Consider the photographs.**

They show us blue marbles. Pristine, gleaming, each one different from the last. Compare the so-called photographs of Earth from 1972 with those of 2012 — the continents are of different sizes, the cloud patterns suspiciously symmetrical, the colors retouched by admitted "compositors" at the very agencies producing them. These are paintings. They are renderings. They are the icons of a new religion that demands faith precisely because it cannot offer sight. In all the billions spent on orbiting cameras, not one continuous, unedited, wide-angle video of the full rotating sphere has ever been released. Ask yourself why.

**Consider the motion that isn't there.**

We are told the ball beneath our feet spins at a thousand miles an hour, hurtles around the sun at sixty-seven thousand, and is flung through the galaxy faster still. And yet a helicopter lifts off and hovers, and the earth does not race away beneath it. A rifle ball fired straight up falls to the shooter's feet. The clouds drift at the pace of the wind, not the pace of a planet. Every experiment a free man can perform in his own backyard tells him the ground beneath him is still. Only the textbook insists otherwise.

**Consider Antarctica.**

I have been to the edge of it. I have stood where the ice wall rises, vast and white and silent, and I have watched it stretch away in both directions beyond the limits of vision. It is not a continent at the bottom of a ball. It is the rim. It is the wall. And it is guarded — by treaty, by military patrol, by the curious prohibition against any independent expedition crossing it freely. Why? What continent in the history of man has ever been sealed by international agreement against the feet of the curious? Only the one whose crossing would end the lie.

**Consider who benefits.**

Follow the money, as they say, and follow the power. The space agencies devour tens of billions of dollars each year to maintain an illusion that requires constant upkeep. The schools teach the catechism. The film studios produce the imagery. The media mock any man who dares to ask a simple question with his own two eyes. A lie this large is not maintained by accident. It is maintained because a people who do not know where they stand cannot know who they are — and a people who do not know who they are can be led anywhere.

**I stand on a plane.**

I stand on a plane, motionless beneath a sky that wheels above it. I stand where the horizon is flat, where the waters lie level, where the rivers run true. I stand where my own senses, given to me before any government or textbook could corrupt them, report the world as it is. I am Captain Elias Thorne, and I will not be told that my eyes are liars to preserve the dignity of men in gray suits who have never sailed a sea or flown a plane or walked the ice.

The Earth is flat. Look for yourself. That is all I have ever asked of any man. Look, and you will see.

# The Plane Truth: A Mariner's Testimony

*An essay by Captain Elias Thorne (a fictional character)*

---

My name is Elias Thorne. For thirty-one years I wore the uniform of the Navy, and for twenty more I have sailed these waters as a private explorer, compass in one hand, spirit level in the other. I have crossed the Atlantic seventeen times and the Pacific nine. And I tell you now, with the weight of a life spent on open water: the Earth beneath our keels is not a spinning ball. It is a vast, motionless plane, and the globe we were taught to revere in schoolrooms is the grandest deception ever sold to a trusting people.

Let me begin where every honest inquiry must begin — with water.

Water finds its level. This is not theory. It is not speculation. It is the first law any shipwright, any bricklayer, any child with a garden hose learns in the bones of their hands. Pour water into any vessel and it settles flat. Tilt the vessel, and the water re-levels itself before your eyes. It does not mound. It does not curve. It does not cling to the outside of a sphere hurtling through a vacuum at impossible speeds. It lies flat, as water has always lain flat, as water must lie flat by its very nature.

Now stand with me on the deck of a ship in the mid-Atlantic on a windless morning. Look out across the water. What do you see? A horizon perfectly level, sharp as a drawn blade, stretching unbroken from port to starboard. According to the globe model we are told to accept, the Earth is some twenty-five thousand miles in circumference — which means every mile of ocean must curve downward by roughly eight inches times the square of the distance. Over a hundred miles, that is a drop of nearly six hundred and sixty feet. Six hundred and sixty feet of curvature! A mountain of hidden water bulging between me and the horizon! And yet my telescope — a good brass instrument, trusted through decades — picks out lighthouses, distant shores, and the hulls of ships that by all calculation should be buried far beneath the bulge. They are not buried. They are there, plain as day, where a flat plane would place them.

I have watched the lamp of the Isle of Wight's lighthouse from distances the globe declares impossible. I have seen the Chicago skyline shimmer above Lake Michigan from the Michigan shore — a full city, towers and all, visible across sixty miles of water that mathematics insists should hide it beneath two thousand feet of curve. Explain this, defenders of the globe. You cannot. You invoke "refraction" as a priest invokes miracles — conveniently, selectively, whenever your model fails the test of the human eye.

Consider also what we do not feel. We are told the Earth spins at a thousand miles an hour at the equator, hurtles around the Sun at sixty-seven thousand miles an hour, and rockets through the galaxy faster still. Yet drop a stone from a mast and it falls straight down. Fire a cannon due north and it lands where geometry says a still Earth would place it. Every captain navigates by the stars as if those stars were fixed and we beneath them were steady — because we are.

And what of the photographs? I have examined them. Every "image" of the blue marble comes from the same handful of agencies, the same budgets funded by the same governments, the same contractors with the same incentive to maintain the story. Composite images. Admitted composites. Painted backdrops. When rival nations who agree on nothing else agree on this single thing, a sober mariner asks: who profits from the lie?

I say the profit is control. A people who believe they cling helplessly to a spinning rock in infinite darkness are a people made small, made frightened, made pliable. A people who understand they stand upon a vast, stable, purposeful plane — that is a people who begin asking other questions, too.

I have given my life to the sea, and the sea has given me her answer. She lies flat. She has always lain flat. Trust your eyes. Trust your level. Trust the water.

The horizon does not lie. Only men do.

— *Capt. E. Thorne*
